meta_title: For Students: Antigravity project template to Solve can't deploy apps (Intermediate Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome can't deploy apps. A Intermediate-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Antigravity Template to Solve Deployment Issues
content.seo_description: Learn how to use the Antigravity project template and Claude Code to overcome deployment hurdles. A professional guide for students building their first apps.
content.answer_block: Stop fighting server configurations. The Antigravity project template abstracts infrastructure complexity into a single, declarative file. By pairing it with Claude Code, you automate the deployment pipeline, ensuring your code moves from local development to a live, scalable production environment without manual intervention or complex DevOps overhead.
content.intro:
You have spent weeks perfecting your logic, polishing your UI, and squashing bugs, only to hit the 'deployment wall.' The silence of a broken build or a 502 Bad Gateway error is the initiation rite of every developer. It is the moment where code stops being a hobby and starts being a product.

But you don't need a degree in systems architecture to ship. By leveraging the Antigravity project template, you gain a production-ready blueprint that handles the heavy lifting of containerization and cloud provisioning. Combined with the precision of Claude Code, you aren't just writing code—you are architecting a professional-grade deployment pipeline.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Scaffold
steps.description: Establish the root architecture. This step creates the standardized environment required for seamless cloud handoffs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new project scaffold using the Antigravity template for a TypeScript-based web application with automated CI/CD pipeline integration.

steps.title: Configure Infrastructure as Code
steps.description: Define your cloud resources within the Antigravity config. This ensures your local environment matches your production environment exactly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a declarative infrastructure configuration for a scalable serverless backend and a PostgreSQL database instance.

steps.title: Inject Deployment Logic
steps.description: Use Claude Code to automate the build scripts that Antigravity requires for your specific framework.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a build script that optimizes assets and bundles the application for production deployment.

steps.title: Validate Environment Variables
steps.description: Securely map your secrets to the deployment environment to prevent runtime failures during the initial push.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a secure schema for environment variables and ensure the deployment pipeline injects them correctly.

steps.title: Execute Automated Deployment
steps.description: Trigger the final deployment cycle to bridge the gap between your local terminal and the live web.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Trigger the Antigravity deployment sequence and monitor the health check endpoints for success.

pro_tips: Always use environment-specific config files to avoid accidentally hitting your production database during testing.
Enable 'dry-run' mode in your deployment scripts to visualize infrastructure changes before they incur costs.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your deployment pipeline, not just your application code.
Keep your build artifacts small; use tree-shaking and asset compression to ensure sub-second deployment times.
Implement health-check pings in your Antigravity config to automatically rollback if a deployment fails.
content.next_steps: Once your first deployment is live, monitor your logs for 24 hours and begin implementing a custom domain and SSL certificate to professionalize your portfolio project.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools, the template, and the roadmap. Stop dreaming about your app and start letting the world use it. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
